title: Integrate Sleepy Mode (Stein's autonomous Claude Code loop) for long-horizon unattended sprints
source_context: User has Claude Pro Max account with unused token headroom. Wants autonomous overnight/weekend loops that burn allocated tokens on real work while Elmo sleeps. Stein's sleepy repo is MIT-licensed — can vendor/fork and adapt to Rick's Morty/ticket model. Key insight: our .cto/tickets/ is already a backlog; add a sleepy_supervisor script that loops orchestrate sprint with token budget tracking + worktree isolation + review queue. [PRIORITY: user-requested Pro Max token utilization]
description: User's colleague Stein (github.com/STEIN64-BIT/sleepy, MIT) built an autonomous Claude Code loop that runs unattended for hours/days producing git branches for review. Core mechanics: (1) supervisor process enforces duration + token cap (--cap 5000000) + intensity presets (30k-300k tokens/iter), (2) orchestrator session decides next task writing next-action.json, (3) worker session executes in isolated git worktree on sleepy/<slug> branch, commits + writes SLEEPY_ARTIFACT.md, (4) diff validated against deny-list, (5) completed work queued in SLEEPY_QUEUE.md for review. Adaptive pacing downshifts intensity (high->medium->low) if burn rate exceeds remaining budget. PERFECT FIT with Rick's architecture — our Rick=orchestrator, Morty=worker, tickets=backlog, .cto/=.sleepy/. Unlocks: (a) Elmo's Pro Max token allowance fully utilized in sleepy mode overnight runs, (b) git worktree isolation (new safety layer we lack), (c) Opus 4.7 task_budget integration perfectly complements Sleepy's adaptive pacing.
proposed_changes: Create scripts/sleepy.py with subcommands: (1) 'sleepy go <english>' — NL planner using claude -p --model opus-4-7 to set duration/focus; (2) 'sleepy start --duration 8h --cap 5M --intensity medium' — runs orchestrate sprint loop in a subprocess, each ticket gets worktree, commits to sleepy/TICKET-id branch; (3) 'sleepy queue' — show pending review items; (4) 'sleepy apply N' / 'sleepy discard N' — merge or drop. Store state in .cto/sleepy/ (RULES.md, BACKLOG.md symlinked to tickets, QUEUE.md, budget.json). Integrate with Opus 4.7 task_budget: adaptive pacing writes task_budget=remaining_tokens/expected_iters to each Morty delegation. Add denylist validation reusing security_utils.py. Log iterations to .cto/logs/sleepy-{date}.jsonl.
notes: Auto-evolve timed out twice on greenfield scaffolding; built MVP manually. 585-line scripts/sleepy.py + .cto/sleepy/ scaffolding.
